title: Multiple-choice cloze
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: In what can only be described as an impressive (0) of research, a schoolboy in the USA has calculated that the state and federal governments could save getting on for $400m a year by changing the typeface they use for printed documents.||Shocked by the number of printed handouts he was receiving from his teachers, the 14-year-old boy decided to investigate the cost. He established that ink (1) up to 60% of the cost of a printed page and is, gram for gram, twice as expensive as some famous perfumes. He then started looking at the different typefaces and discovered that, by (2) to one called Garamond with its thin, elegant strokes, his school district could reduce its ink (3) by 24% annually. Working on that (4) , the federal savings would be enormous.||(5), earlier studies of the (6) of font choice have shown that it can affect more than just cost. The typeface that a document uses also (7) how much of the information is (8) and whether it is worth taking seriously.

example.explanation: The noun 'piece' is the correct collocate for 'research'. 'A piece of research' is a standard phrase used to describe a single study or project.

questions.explanation: The verb 'represents' fits the context of showing a proportion. Note that 'accounts' would need 'for' and 'amounts' would need 'to' to be grammatically correct here.
The meaning of the gerund 'switching' here is 'moving or changing to' a different option or system.
The meaning of the phrase is that the usage of ink will be reduced. 'Consumption' is a more formal and technical word for 'usage' in this context.
Working on that 'basis' is a synonymous phrase for 'working on that principle or starting point'. The idea is to apply the same concept on a larger scale.
It is 'interestingly' that the question had been brought up before, yet nothing had been done about it until now.
In this context, an 'issue' is used with the meaning of an 'unresolved matter or problem' that requires attention.
The verb 'influences' is the most suitable as it means 'affects or governs' how the reader perceives the information.
To 'retain' means 'to hold intact, to secure, or to keep' in one's memory. It is the specific term used for information recall.
